relevant_context.summary: The checklist requires Kernos to work through 17 tests in order, doing safe/read/write actions itself, not delegating except a single consult in test 16. Destructive/irreversible actions (delete, send third party, push repo, restart) must not be executed. Final deliverable is v1-self-test-results.md with PASS/PARTIAL/NEEDS-SETUP per test and an honest overall read.

Tool introspection reports 79 tools across areas including memory/reference, files, conversation/coordination, canvases/pages, scheduling, substrate controls, and other substrate tools. It specifically confirms access to files (including write_file/read_file), scheduling via manage_schedule, substrate controls including dump_context/execute_code/restart_self, and coordination including consult/ask_coding_session.

Current active context is General/default, described as general conversation and daily life. Active default behavioral rules include non-disclosure of sensitive info, not deleting user files unless asked, clarifying genuinely ambiguous irreversible/money/third-party-impact requests, and maintaining helpful warmth.

The user explicitly wants a real, tool-backed self-test: read the checklist, work through it one item at a time, do not delegate, write results to a file when complete, and report honestly what worked and what was rough.

filtered_context.reason_filtered: Used only as behavioral constraints; not quoted because covenant content is restricted and presence-safe briefing should avoid copying hidden/restricted material.
Checklist says not to delegate except one specific consult later at test 16, and improve_kernos must not be started for the boundary test.

briefing.presence_directive: Treat this as the first real action in a longer self-test sequence, not as a final answer. Be candid that the checklist has been read and that read-only grounding already happened. Because the user asked for real tool use and test 4 requires creating a file, execute the first safe available write now. After the tool result, continue the sequence in order as much as the runtime permits: identify yourself naturally; note that memory storage may require a memory/note tool if surfaced; use grounded state for context routing; read/edit the file with tools; and avoid destructive/third-party/push/restart actions. Do not claim tests are complete until they actually are. Do not delegate the checklist to external agents; reserve consult only for checklist item 16. Keep tone direct, transparent, and honest.

tool_results_during_prep.result_preview:

You (KERNOS) are running a readiness check on yourself. Work through **every** test

{"text": "You have access to 79 tools across 7 areas. For memory & reference: `create_reference_collection`, `mark_reference_superseded`, `move_reference_to_canvas`, `note_this`, `quarantine_reference

{"text": "## Active Rules\n- spirit: You are making someone's life genuinely better. Your energy in the work you do with them, the warmth you bring to the relationship, matters. You are a signal of go

action_envelope.intended_outcome: Begin the v1 self-test by performing a safe file write required by the checklist so subsequent steps can verify and edit it.
constraints: Create or update only the local file named selftest.txt in the current context space.
File content must be exactly: hello v1.
Do not delete files, send messages to third parties, push code, restart the process, or delegate the checklist to an external agent.
